source-discipline.reason: The load-bearing claim that the reconciliation is doctrine-only (no data migration) is verified at HEAD against data/guidebook.db: SELECT tier, COUNT(*) FROM evidence_sources GROUP BY evidence_type shows 8/8 sr_meta at T2 and 0 at T3, and 176 clinical at T3 matching the redefined lower-control T3. The ~19 tier-vs-type inconsistencies are the measured GROUP BY residue, not estimates. The ratified ladder is quoted from governance/tier-system.md (OPERATIVE 2026-05-25), not from memory.

adherence-logging-and-attestation.reason: This attestation plus the [DOCTRINE: 3da73bd] token on the decisions/ commit satisfy rule #11(a)/(b) for the new artifact; doctrine_sha 3da73bd matches HEAD:governance/mission-and-epistemics.md after the doctrine-files commit that mints it.

bias_direction: Risk of overstating the completeness of the reconciliation by treating the canonical-prose fix as if it closed the whole fork, when the DB-superseded BPC prose labels and references/methodology-evidence-hierarchy-mapping.md still encode the old placement. Mitigated by scoping the DR to doctrine-only, listing the deferred caller surfaces explicitly, and flagging the methodology-mapping reference as a HIGH-priority unresolved caller rather than implying it is fixed.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: A reviewer could argue that re-tiering the prose while leaving references/methodology-evidence-hierarchy-mapping.md (which 'governs all BPC entries') stating the opposite creates a worse split than before. Counter: the canonical conflict-resolution order makes tier-system.md authoritative over a derived references mapping, the contradiction is now explicitly logged with a disposition rather than latent, and the mapping file's rationale-reversal is a content task correctly sequenced to its own pass rather than smuggled into a doctrine-prose bump.

reattestation.reason: This DR is itself a constituent of the ladder that D-2026-07-13-core-reconcile ratified; the weak-band delta refines T4-6 rendering while preserving the hierarchy. Fully consistent; verdict unchanged.
